name: Cliff’s Edge (Lunar Verge)
description: You stand at a cliff above a vast, glassy ocean. The moon hangs too large, too close, illuminating endless possibilities and endless endings.

characters.name: Moon Watcher
characters.backstory: A figure who never looks away from the moon. Sometimes, they weep silver tears.

encounters.question: Does the moon pull differently since the world tilted?

responses.text: The pull's the same and we're what's different. Gravity's an honest merchant; it's our bones that learned new ways to ache. I measure the discrepancy in tide tables written on my spine.
Tilted implies there was ever a correct angle. The moon pulls what it always pulled and dreams, blood, the silver thread between sanity and howling. We just feel it more now that we're mostly hollow.

encounters.question: Have you counted all its faces, or do new ones keep appearing?

responses.text: Twenty-eight faces in the classical count, forty-three since the atmosphere thinned. Each one remembers something we've forgotten and I'm cataloguing them before they turn away completely. The newest one looks like regret wearing pearls.
Counting assumes they hold still. I've seen the same face become stranger between heartbeats, familiar craters rearranging when I dare to breathe. Better to witness than quantify.

encounters.question: Will you teach me the patience to watch without wanting?

responses.text: Wanting's the first thing you feed to the vigil. Then hope. Then fear of forgetting. What's left sits cleaner than meditation and you become a recording device for light that traveled centuries to die in your retina. I'll show you the posture if you're serious.
Patience isn't taught; it's eroded into you. Each night scrapes away another layer of urgency until you're bare nerve watching bare sky. Come back when you've forgotten what you came for.

characters.name: The Diver
characters.backstory: Claims they can show you what lies beneath, but has never climbed back up.

encounters.question: Do you calculate the distance, or does falling feel like faith?

responses.text: Faith's for people who expect to land somewhere specific. I calculate in increments of transformation and this many meters until the wind rewrites your skeleton, that many until gravity becomes a love song. It's very precise abandonment.

Distance is just time wearing a different mask. I measure in the weight of almost and almost ready, almost free, almost the person I was before I learned edges were optional. Math ruins the mythology.

encounters.question: Is the water patient, or does it hunger like the height?

responses.text: Hunger's too simple and the water practices a kind of liquid mathematics, solving for the variable of your velocity. It's been calculating your arrival since before you were born. Patient implies it could choose otherwise.
Water's just gravity in a different costume. The height promises transformation; the water delivers it. Neither patient nor hungry and just inevitable, like the way your cells remember being ocean.

encounters.question: Will you jump today, or are you still perfecting the approach?

responses.text: Today's jump lives in my muscles already and I've fallen a thousand times between breakfast and this conversation. The approach is the art; the leap's just punctuation. Watch my shoulders for the tell.

Perfection's a leash that keeps you on the ledge. I jump constantly in the space between thoughts. What you're seeing is just the rehearsal bleeding through. The real dive happens where you can't follow.
